description_long: This is a vintage box-style television with a woodgrain-patterned exterior casing and a prominent convex glass screen. The front panel features two large rotary dials for channel and volume control, as well as a speaker grille and a small brand label. The television's body is rectangular, with the back section protruding in a trapezoidal shape to accommodate the cathode ray tube. The sides and top are finished in a dark, polished wood veneer, while the front bezel is metallic with black and silver accents. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century consumer electronics. The object appears heavy and solid, with no visible modern ports or flat-panel features.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain exterior, convex glass screen, and large rotary dials on the metallic front panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, two large rotary dials, speaker grille, and brand label.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the uninterrupted woodgrain panel and the protruding back section.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the black, trapezoidal back housing for the cathode ray tube and ventilation details.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the woodgrain finish and the depth of the back housing.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick metallic bezel with black and silver accents, rectangular shape, and woodgrain side trim.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of two large rotary dials and a button on the front right panel
